content.problemStatement:
Chef goes to the supermarket to buy some items. Luckily there's a sale going on under which Chef gets the following offer:


If Chef buys 333 items then he gets the item (out of those 333 items) having the lowest price as free.
For e.g. if Chef bought 333 items with the cost 666, 222 and 444, then he would get the item with cost 222 as free. So he would only have to pay the cost of the other two items which will be 6+4=106 + 4 = 106+4=10.

Chef buys 333 items having prices AAA, BBB and CCC respectively. What is the amount of money Chef needs to pay?


Input Format

The first line will contain an integer TTT - number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains three integers A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C - the prices of the items bought by Chef.


Output Format
For each test case, output the price paid by Chef.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains three space‑separated integers A, B and C – the prices of the three items Chef buys.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total amount Chef has to pay after getting the cheapest of the three items for free.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Prices are 6, 2 and 4. The cheapest (2) is free, so Chef pays 6+4=10.\nTest case 2: All prices are 3, one of them becomes free, Chef pays 3+3=6.\nTest case 3: Prices are 8, 4 and 4. The cheapest price is 4, Chef pays 8+4=12.
First test: sum=15, min=1, pay=14. Second test: sum=21, min=1, pay=20.

hidden.label: edge case with two equal lows

solution.approach: For each test case we need the sum of the three prices and subtract the smallest price, because that item becomes free. The smallest price can be obtained with the built‑in min function or a simple comparison. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        a = int(next(it)); b = int(next(it)); c = int(next(it))
        ans = a + b + c - min(a, b, c)
        out.append(str(ans))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
